session.description: Gel finger touch test on CH5(F7/yellow wire) and CH6(F8/orange wire), SRB on both earlobes with gel, no BIAS connected

thresholds.note: DC-corrected railing (mean subtracted before threshold check)

observations.cyton_vs_daisy: Cyton channels (CH1-8) show high 60Hz pickup (antenna effect) when floating. Daisy channels (CH9-16) tend to bias-pin with near-zero 60Hz and moderate std, producing false-yellow readings.
observations.touch_response: Gel finger contact on F7/F8 drops rail% to 0, std from ~1600 to ~70-125 uV, and 60Hz varies 0-80% depending on reference quality. Best contact on F7 reached std=48.8 (briefly GREEN).
observations.reference_quality: SRB earclip with gel provides adequate but noisy reference. Without BIAS driven ground, common-mode rejection is poor — all channels inherit ~100-200 uV residual noise floor even with good electrode contact.
observations.bias_needed: GREEN status requires BIAS connection to cancel common-mode noise. SRB-only reference caps best-case at YELLOW (~50-200 uV std).
observations.false_yellows: Daisy channels T7, T8, P3, P4 show persistent YELLOW despite no contact — bias-pinned with std 50-170 uV and 0% 60Hz. A suspiciously-clean check (0% 60Hz + moderate std) could catch this.
observations.channel_correlation: Cyton channels are strongly correlated (shared reference path). Daisy channels correlate internally but weakly with Cyton. Cross-board independence suggests separate analog paths.
